[[Don Mann]] (uncle) }} }} '''George William Mann'''{{cite web|url=http://nzleague.co.nz/kiwis/player_profile.php?letter=M&id=620|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120402171110/http://nzleague.co.nz/kiwis/player_profile.php?letter=M&id=620|url-status=dead|archive-date=2 April 2012|title=MANN, GEORGE WILLIAM 1989 - 91 - KIWI #620|publisher=nzleague.co.nz|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}} (born 31 July 1965) is a New Zealand-Tongan former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s and 1990s, who represented both [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]]. ==Background== George Mann is the cousin of the rugby league footballer; [[Duane Mann]].Coffey and Wood ''The Kiwis: 100 Years of International Rugby League'' {{ISBN|1-86971-090-8}} ==Playing career== A [[Mangere East Hawks|Mangere East]] junior, Mann played for [[Auckland rugby league team|Auckland]], [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]], [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]],{{cite web|title=George Mann|url=http://www.therhinos.co.uk/rugby/matchcentre/leeds_rhinos_1st_xiii_profiles.php?player=84036&includeref=dynamic|publisher=Leeds Rhinos|access-date=18 February 2014|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140222174226/http://www.therhinos.co.uk/rugby/matchcentre/leeds_rhinos_1st_xiii_profiles.php?player=84036&includeref=dynamic|archive-date=22 February 2014}} [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] and [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]].Frank Endacott with John Coffey ''Being Frank: The Frank Endacott Story''. Auckland, Hodder Moa Beckett, 2002. {{ISBN|1-86958-922-X}}. p.88 ===County Cup Final appearances=== Mann played as a {{rlp|sr}} and scored two [[Try (rugby)|tries]] in [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]' 24–14 victory over [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[1991 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1991–92 Rugby Football League season|1991–92 season]] at [[Wilderspool Stadium]], [[Warrington]], on Sunday 20 October 1991. He also played in St. Helens' 4–5 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1992 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93 season]] at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]], on Sunday 18 October 1992. ===International honours=== Mann was a [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] international and played for Tonga at the [[1986 Pacific Cup]] and [[1995 Rugby League World Cup|1995 World Cup]].{{cite book |last =John Coffey, Bernie Wood |title = 100 years: Māori rugby league, 1908-2008 |publisher =Huia Publishers |year = 2008 |pages = 224–226 |url = https://books.google.com/books?id=nklWo8vw-iIC |isbn =978-1-86969-331-2}} ==Retirement== His career was ended in 2000 by a high tackle from [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]]'s [[Chris Holland (rugby league)|Chris Holland]], Mann suffered horrific facial injuries, including a broken jaw.{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/819734.stm|title=Holland awarded lengthy ban|publisher=news.bbc.co.uk|date=4 July 2000|access-date=1 January 2018}} ==References== {{reflist}}
